useCases.challenge: Child spent 3+ hours daily playing passive mobile games with no creation.
useCases.outcome: Shifted to active creation. Built and shared 4 custom arcade games with school friends, understanding variables and loops.

useCases.challenge: Extremely slow typing speed (under 10 WPM), hunting-and-pecking keys with one finger.
useCases.outcome: Learned correct home row finger alignment and posture. Speed increased to 35+ WPM with 95% accuracy in 6 weeks.

useCases.challenge: Child struggled to design neat PowerPoint presentations for school projects, spending hours on manual styling.
useCases.outcome: Learned slide masters, design templates, and speaking structure. Built a science project deck in under 30 minutes.

useCases.challenge: High school math and science data assignments were messy; child didn't know how to organize numbers.
useCases.outcome: Mastered grid structures, arithmetic formulas (=SUM, =AVERAGE), and automatic column charts to visualize lab experiments.

useCases.challenge: Parent was worried about online safety, weak password habits, and potential exposure to online gaming scams.
useCases.outcome: Learned password encryption rules, phishing link detection, browser privacy settings, and two-factor authentication.

useCases.challenge: Curious child kept asking about ChatGPT and AI, but parents wanted a safe, structured introduction.
useCases.outcome: Learned prompt engineering frameworks (Role, Task, Context) and safe data boundaries, using AI for writing support and quiz generation.

useCases.challenge: Lack of local computer coaching centers offering modern, practical computer skills instead of theory textbooks.
useCases.outcome: Learned file management directories, Google Docs margins, index creation, and shared cloud folder collaboration.

useCases.challenge: Child found school computer labs boring and theory-based, losing interest in computers.
useCases.outcome: Discovered hardware shortcuts (Ctrl+C, Ctrl+V, Alt+Tab), custom folder organization, and desktop customizations through gamified tasks.

useCases.challenge: Student wanted to learn coding, but local school didn't have a structured syllabus or coding clubs.
useCases.outcome: Built a coordinate-based math maze game. Presented it virtually to the entire class, boosting public confidence.

useCases.challenge: High school student wanted to build a digital skill portfolio to prepare for competitive college applications.
useCases.outcome: Created a unified digital portfolio showcasing a resume, Scratch game link, and Excel data sheet, gaining tech confidence.
